title: Apply — Future Caribbean Global AI Buildathon
description: Apply to the Future Caribbean Global AI Buildathon. 40 teams, 10 tracks, real deployment. Applications close June 10, 2026.
text: Apply — Future Caribbean Global AI Buildathon FUTUR CARIBB AN The Opportunity How It Works Tracks Judges Alumni Prizes FAQ Partner Contact Apply Now The Opportunity How It Works Tracks Judges Alumni Prizes FAQ Partner Contact Apply Now Application Apply to the Buildathon 40 teams. 10 tracks. Three weeks to build something real. Fill out the form below and we will review your application. 40 Teams Selected globally across 10 challenge tracks $50K Cash Prizes Plus OWC AI deployment systems H200 GPU Compute NVIDIA H200-class infrastructure for all 50 participants NYSE Pitch Top 3 teams pitch live at the NYSE Don't fill this: Basic Information 1. Full Name * Full name is required 2. Preferred / Display Name 3. Email Address * A valid email is required 4. Phone Number Include country code 5. Country of Residence * Country is required 6. Nationality 7. LinkedIn Profile 8. X / Twitter Profile 9. GitHub Profile 10. Personal Website / Portfolio 11. Discord Username 12. WhatsApp Number * WhatsApp number is required Professional Background 13. Current Role / Title 14. Current Company / Organization 15. Years of Experience Select range 0 – 1 year 2 – 4 years 5 – 9 years 10+ years 16. Primary Skillset * Select primary skill AI / ML Engineer Software Engineer Full Stack Developer Product Manager Designer Researcher Entrepreneur Operations Climate / Energy Specialist Finance Specialist Healthcare Specialist Creative / Artist Real Estate / Infrastructure Other Primary skillset is required 17. Secondary Skillsets 18. Technical Stack Experience 19. Have you worked with AI or agentic AI systems before? * Yes No Please select an option 20. Have you participated in a hackathon or buildathon before? Yes No 21. Have you launched a startup, product, or company before? Yes No Buildathon Track Selection 22. Which track are you applying for? * 01 — AI for Tourism & Transportation Agentic systems for travel, logistics, hospitality, guest experience, transportation coordination, concierge systems, and regional mobility infrastructure. 02 — Finance, Payments & MSME Capital Cross-border payments, regional financial infrastructure, AI underwriting, SME financing, remittances, compliance systems, digital banking, and capital coordination. 03 — Healthcare Systems & Delivery Telemedicine, patient coordination, chronic disease management, diagnostics, healthcare operations, AI triage systems, and medical infrastructure. 04 — Climate Risk & Disaster Coordination Disaster response systems, climate intelligence, emergency logistics, hurricane preparedness, resilience coordination, and risk forecasting. 05 — Energy, Climate & Resilience Grid optimization, renewable energy systems, microgrids, climate adaptation, infrastructure resilience, forecasting, and distributed energy coordination. 06 — Ocean Systems & Blue Economy Ocean intelligence systems, fisheries, marine protection, coastal monitoring, blue economy infrastructure, maritime logistics, and environmental coordination. 07 — Food Systems & Supply Chains Agriculture, food security, supply chain optimization, farm-to-market systems, yield forecasting, trade logistics, and distribution infrastructure. 08 — AI for The Arts Creative AI, storytelling systems, music, film, digital experiences, creator tooling, cultural preservation, entertainment infrastructure, and AI-native artistic workflows. 09 — AI for Real Estate & Development Property systems, urban planning, construction coordination, infrastructure intelligence, smart buildings, housing systems, and development optimization. 10 — Open Track Frontier ideas that do not fit neatly into the other tracks but demonstrate strong potential for real-world impact, scalability, or novel technological innovation. Please select a track 23. Why did you choose this track? * Please explain your track choice 24. What real-world problem are you trying to solve? * Please describe the problem 25. Describe your proposed solution or idea * Please describe your proposed solution 26. Why does this matter for the Caribbean or the world? * Please explain why this matters 27. If your solution succeeds, what could it become in 5 years? Team Information 28. Are you applying: * Solo As a Team Looking to Join a Team Please select how you are applying 29. Team Name 30. Team Members + Roles 31. Would you be open to being matched with other participants to form a team? Yes No 32. If yes, what type of teammates are you looking for? 33. Would you be comfortable collaborating with international participants across multiple time zones? Yes No 34. Are you interested in continuing the project after the Buildathon if traction develops? Yes No Maybe Technical & Operational Information 35. What tools, models, APIs, or frameworks are you planning to use? 36. Have you worked with any of the following? OpenClaw Open-source AI frameworks Multi-agent systems LLM orchestration APIs & integrations Cloud infrastructure NVIDIA tooling Vector databases AI deployment systems None of the above 37. Will your project require compute resources? Yes No 38. Estimated compute intensity Select intensity Low Medium High Frontier-scale experimentation 39. Will your project require external datasets? Yes No If yes, please specify 40. Will your project involve sensitive or private data? Yes No If yes, please specify Video Submission Please upload a Loom video (2–5 minutes) introducing yourself and your project idea. Cover: who you are, your background, why you want to participate, the problem you want to solve, why your idea matters, and why your team should be selected. 42. Loom Video Link Loom video link is required Availability & Commitment 43. Can you commit to the full 3-week build phase? * Yes No Please confirm your availability 44. Estimated hours you can contribute per week 45. Are you available for live check-ins, mentorship sessions, workshops, and demo presentations? Yes No 46. If selected as a finalist, would you be willing to travel to Barbados for the finals? Yes No Maybe Startup & Venture Potential 47. Have you raised venture funding before? Yes No 48. Are you interested in turning this into a startup or long-term company? Yes No Maybe 49. Would you be interested in future incubation, advisory support, partnerships, or investment opportunities connected to FutureCaribbean and ACTAI Advisors? Yes No Maybe Additional Information 50. Anything else you would like us to know? Legal & Consent 51. Do you agree to the Buildathon rules and code of conduct? * Yes No You must agree to the rules and code of conduct 52. Do you consent to FutureCaribbean publicly sharing your project, demo, team information, and related content? * Yes No Please indicate your consent 53. Do you consent to being contacted by mentors, sponsors, partners, investors, media, and ecosystem participants connected to the Buildathon? * Yes No Please indicate your consent 54. Do you understand that participation does not guarantee investment, funding, employment, or commercial deployment? * Yes No Please acknowledge this disclaimer Submit Application Application Received Thank you for applying to the Future Caribbean Global AI Buildathon. We will review your application and email you by June 17 if your team is selected. Back to Home © 2026 Future Caribbean. All rights reserved.
